title: Hims Weight Loss 2026: Cost, Pros and Cons, and More
content:
Hims is a telehealth service providing online consultations and prescription medications for weight loss, hair loss, sexual health, and more.

It specializes in men’s health, while its sister brand, Hers, focuses on women’s health.

The weight loss plan is designed to support weight maintenance with glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) medications.

All Hims services are provided online. You won’t speak with a healthcare professional in person or in real time.

»Learn about how we vet online GLP-1 providers.

Hims Weight Loss offers the following GLP-1 medications:

Available GLP-1 forms include pills, injection pens, vials, and KwikPens.

Hims offers continuous support and monitoring through its app and online platform. The app allows you to message a healthcare professional to discuss your progress and adjust your plan as needed.

The app also contains educational content on lifestyle changes, nutrition, and exercise for weight loss and maintenance.

Hims uses an encrypted data platform to keep user information private.

During sign-up, you will have to provide personal information, such as your medical history. You will also need to provide a government-issued ID and a credit card to access Hims Weight Loss.

The intake process for Hims weight loss is designed to be quick and simple. It involves the following steps:

The cost of GLP-1 medications varies depending on the type of medication and form. Current prices per month are as follows:

Hims Weight Loss is a membership plan requiring monthly payments. This does not include the cost of GLP-1 medications.

Membership starts at $39 for the first month. After, it increases to $149 per month.

Members can take advantage of partner offers and discounts, such as 10% off a first order of Hims meal replacement products.

Hims does not accept insurance. This means all payments must be out of pocket.

Customers may use their health savings accounts (HSAs)/flexible spending accounts (FSAs) to request reimbursement.

Hims Weight Loss membership starts at $39 for the first month, and $149 per month thereafter.

This does not include the cost of GLP-1 medications, which can range from $149 to $1,899 per month, depending on the type and form.

Hims Weight Loss membership starts at $39 for the first month, and $149 per month thereafter.

This does not include the cost of GLP-1 medications, which can range from $149 to $1,899 per month, depending on the type and form.

Hims is a legitimate telehealth business that offers a range of healthcare services for men. Hims Weight Loss offers access to legitimate GLP-1 medication prescriptions.

Hims is a legitimate telehealth business that offers a range of healthcare services for men. Hims Weight Loss offers access to legitimate GLP-1 medication prescriptions.

Alternatives to Hims for Weight Loss for people seeking safe, quick online access to GLP-1 medications include Ro Body, Noom Med, and Sesame Care.

Alternatives to Hims for Weight Loss for people seeking safe, quick online access to GLP-1 medications include Ro Body, Noom Med, and Sesame Care.

Hims Weight Loss offers quick, convenient online consultations and prescriptions for GLP-1 medications. The app provides access to messaging with healthcare professionals and educational resources on weight loss.

Hims Weight Loss may suit people seeking an easy, digital-only service for weight loss and management with medication. It may not suit people looking for a weight loss plan that focuses more on lifestyle changes, fitness, and nutrition.

Hims cannot replace in-person care and is not appropriate for more complex or urgent medical needs.

title: Ask the Expert: How to Manage Nutrition Challenges in Metastatic Gastroesophageal Adenocarcinoma
content:
Hematologist/oncologist Dr. Sheel Patel answers questions about managing nutrition during treatment for metastatic gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma. 

Individuals with cancer that is localized to the esophagus or stomach may have nutritional challenges related to the effects of surgery, radiation, or medication during treatment.

Surgical removal of the cancer may require a feeding tube with feeding formula to rest the healing tissues of the esophagus and stomach.

And radiation can cause “radiation esophagitis,” which results in discomfort in the esophageal area, hindering the ability to eat properly.

However, metastatic gastroesophageal cancer is usually not treated with surgery. Radiation to a gastroesophageal mass could be used as a palliative treatment if the mass-related symptoms are significant. When this occurs, the radiation could lead to inflammation of the tissue, hindering the ability to swallow food comfortably.

Gastroesophageal cancer is most commonly treated with systemic therapy, or medication. Medications include chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and targeted therapy. These are most commonly given through an IV drip.

Medications such as 5FU, platinum chemotherapy, trastuzumab, and pembrolizumab can all have side effects.

These side effects could include mucositis, which is an inflammation or ulceration of the mucous membranes. Mucositis causes pain and poor absorption of food throughout the digestive tract.

Metastatic cancer is considered highly metabolic, meaning as it grows and spreads, it hijacks the body’s energy sources. As the body and cancer burn the body’s energy sources (calories) beyond the amount a person can take in, it can cause a deficit in calories and lead to uncontrolled weight loss.

The symptoms of mucositis, nausea, and dysgeusia (a change in taste buds that sometimes causes a metallic taste in the mouth) are common side effects of cancer treatments in metastatic gastroesophageal cancer.

With mucositis, symptoms are dependent on which area of the digestive tract is affected. Mucous membranes line the entire digestive tract from the mouth to the anus.

If it is in the mouth, symptoms can manifest as painful mouth ulcers, which can hinder the ability to chew food comfortably. Mucositis in the esophagus can manifest as pain on swallowing, once again causing pain while trying to eat food.

This inflammation of the mucous membranes can also hinder areas of the digestive tract, which impairs absorption of nutrients by the body, thus resulting in poor overall nutrition.

Nausea is a common effect of cancer treatments and can restrict or hinder a person’s appetite or their desire to eat food, leading to poor nutrition, decreased calorie intake, and weight loss.

Dysgeusia can decrease your desire to eat, leading to a calorie deficit, with eventual weight loss, and poor nutrition.

Foods that contain adequate nutrition for individuals who have trouble eating can be challenging. If swallowing is not a limiting factor, then high calorie, high protein foods are considered the most beneficial.

However, eating solid food by mouth may not always be feasible, given a person’s symptoms. Therefore, high calorie, high protein liquid supplements (such as shakes) are considered very useful in maintaining a person’s nutrition and weight.

If a person has trouble swallowing, a feeding tube may be placed, and a formula is delivered through the person’s feeding tube to obtain adequate nutrition.

The most common signs of serious malnutrition in people with metastatic gastroesophageal cancer are unintentional weight loss, fatigue (lack of energy), decreased activity, and loss of muscle mass (muscle wasting).

Knowing a person’s baseline before the cancer diagnosis is pivotal in identifying these serious signs.

With poor nutritional status comes changes in skin, nails, and hair, as well as issues in cognition.

A discussion with a dietitian about nutrition should occur even prior to the patient showing signs of symptoms, so they can try to stay ahead of the curve.

Prior to starting any treatment for cancer, it is appropriate for a person to discuss the potential obstacles in maintaining their nutrition for as long as they can and what supplemental interventions (such as tube feeding) may be needed further on down the road if the nutritional status worsens despite optimal oral feeding.

Dr. Sheel Patel is an ABMS board certified physician in hematology, oncology, and internal medicine. Dr. Patel is a practicing physician at the Orlando VA Medical Center in Florida.

title: Your Migraine Prevention All-Star Team
content:
You don’t have to rely on just one strategy to prevent migraine attacks. Combining several prevention tactics may increase your chances of reducing migraine episodes and their impact on your daily life.

Migraine affects everyone differently. Your symptoms, triggers, and the treatments that work best for you may not look the same as someone else’s.

That can make prevention feel complicated. But the good news is that several strategies have been shown to help reduce migraine episode frequency and its impact on your quality of life over time.

Think of these approaches as your migraine prevention all-star team. Each one plays a different role, and together they may help you stay one step ahead of migraine. 

Many factors can combine to trigger a migraine episode. That’s why holistic migraine prevention methods work well. A combination of medication and lifestyle changes can give you the best possible prevention.

Migraine attacks can seem random, but your everyday choices make a difference. Actions as simple as drinking a glass of water first thing in the morning can contribute to fewer migraine episodes and greater well-being.

title: Vitaminas y suplementos para la memoria
content:
Gran parte de la evidencia sobre estos posibles suplementos para mejorar la memoria no es muy sólida. Aquí hablamos de lo que indican los estudios clínicos recientes sobre las vitaminas y la pérdida de memoria, y revisamos otras formas de apoyar tu memoria.

Desde hace mucho tiempo, los científicos investigan la relación entre niveles bajos de B12 (cobalamina) y la pérdida de memoria. Sin embargo, si consumes una cantidad adecuada de B12, no hay evidencia suficiente de que consumir más tenga efectos positivos.

La deficiencia de B12 puede deberse a no obtener suficiente vitamina a través de los alimentos o a dificultades para absorberla. Esto puede ocurrir por factores como:

Deberías poder obtener suficiente B12 de forma natural, ya que está presente en alimentos como el pescado y las aves de corral. Los cereales de desayuno fortificados pueden ser una buena opción para quienes siguen una dieta vegetariana. Pregúntale a tu médico si necesitas un suplemento alimenticio para mantener niveles adecuados.

Algunas evidencias sugieren que la vitamina E puede beneficiar la mente y la memoria de las personas mayores. Sin embargo, se necesita más investigación para entender si esta vitamina puede realmente ayudar a ralentizar el deterioro cognitivo asociado a condiciones como la demencia.

En cualquier caso, la deficiencia de vitamina E es poco común, aunque puede presentarse en personas que siguen dietas bajas en grasa.

Entre los alimentos que contienen vitamina E están:

Según una investigación de 2023, la vitamina D podría interferir con distintas vías de señalización cerebral asociadas con enfermedades neurodegenerativas como la enfermedad de Alzheimer o la enfermedad de Parkinson.

Aun así, se necesitan más estudios en células, animales y personas para entender si tomar suplementos de vitamina D realmente puede mejorar la memoria.

Obtienes vitamina D al exponerte al sol y al comer ciertos alimentos, como el pescado graso o los huevos. Además, algunos alimentos, como la leche, pueden estar fortificados con vitamina D.

No obtener suficiente de la vitamina puede causar deficiencia de vitamina D. Por eso, muchas personas toman suplementos, sobre todo en climas fríos.

Una investigación de 2022 sugiere que los omega-3 podrían ayudar a reducir los síntomas del deterioro cognitivo leve, que pueden incluir la pérdida de memoria.

Otro estudio de 2022 también encontró que incluir omega-3 en tu alimentación podría dar como resultado un mejor aprendizaje, memoria, función cognitiva y flujo sanguíneo al cerebro.

El pescado, las algas y los suplementos de aceite de pescado contienen omega-3. Peces como el salmón y la caballa son las fuentes más abundantes de omega-3, que incluyen el ácido docosahexaenoico (DHA, por sus siglas en inglés) y el ácido eicosapentaenoico (EPA, por sus siglas en inglés).

Aun así, se necesita más investigación para determinar si la suplementación con omega-3 puede mejorar realmente el desarrollo de la memoria.

Aunque los estudios sugieren que algunas vitaminas podrían ser beneficiosas para el cerebro, la investigación no establece una relación de causa y efecto con una mejora de la memoria.

Por ejemplo, una investigación de 2023 sugiere que tomar un multivitamínico o un suplemento con varias vitaminas o minerales podría ayudar a mejorar la memoria en personas mayores. Un estudio de 2024 parece respaldar este hallazgo.

Aun así, estos estudios no analizaron el efecto en personas más jóvenes ni evaluaron vitaminas específicas. Eso significa que no está claro qué ingrediente específico de los multivitamínicos provocó el efecto observado.

Una revisión de estudios de 2023 analizó suplementos actualmente disponibles en el mercado que incluyen al menos 1 de 18 ingredientes comunes. La investigación encontró que no había evidencia o que había evidencia limitada de que los siguientes ingredientes apoyen la memoria:

Además, la revisión también encontró que había evidencia limitada sobre la memoria para las vitaminas B12, D y E.

Sin embargo, la revisión sí encontró evidencia más sólida de que los siguientes ingredientes podrían tener un efecto positivo en la memoria:

Es importante saber que la Administración de Alimentos y Medicamentos (FDA, por sus siglas en inglés) no supervisa los suplementos de la misma manera que supervisa los medicamentos. Esto significa que las personas consumidoras son responsables de investigar los ingredientes de cualquier suplemento que deseen tomar.

Dado que ninguna de las investigaciones revisadas es completamente concluyente, conviene hablar con un médico o un dietista antes de probar cualquier vitamina o suplemento comercialmente disponible como una forma de mejorar la memoria.

Aunque algunos suplementos vitamínicos podrían apoyar la función cognitiva tanto en personas jóvenes como en personas mayores, puede ser más valioso obtener las vitaminas a través de los alimentos. Los suplementos pueden ayudar a cubrir carencias, pero consulta con tu médico antes de exceder la ingesta diaria recomendada.

Sin importar tu edad, la mejor manera de abordar el deterioro de la memoria es comer bien y ejercitar el cuerpo y el cerebro. La dieta mediterránea es una buena fuente de todas las vitaminas que el cuerpo necesita.

La dieta mediterránea puede ser una forma de mejorar los síntomas de la enfermedad de Alzheimer, incluida la pérdida de memoria. Las características principales de esta dieta incluyen:

Entre las dietas similares a la dieta mediterránea se encuentran la dieta MIND y la dieta DASH. Ambas dietas reducen la probabilidad de la enfermedad de Alzheimer.

En particular, la dieta MIND enfatiza comer verduras de hoja verde y alimentos de origen vegetal, además de las recomendaciones de alto contenido de proteína y de aceite de oliva propias de la dieta mediterránea.

Siempre que tu médico lo apruebe, tomar ciertas vitaminas o multivitamínicos no debería perjudicar el cerebro. Sin embargo, la evidencia es variable sobre si esto puede ayudar a reducir la pérdida de memoria asociada a la demencia.

Si vives con demencia, contar con una red de apoyo sólida y participar en tu comunidad local pueden ser tan importantes, o incluso más, que establecer hábitos de sueño saludables, que también puede proteger el cerebro.

Por ejemplo, una investigación de 2023 muestra que el ejercicio físico regular puede beneficiar a las personas con Alzheimer y mejorar la memoria y la función cognitiva.

También puedes mejorar la salud del cerebro si prestas más atención a los alimentos y hábitos que se sabe que lo dañan. Por ejemplo, existe un vínculo entre los alimentos fritos y el daño al sistema cardiovascular, lo que afecta la eficiencia del cerebro.

Una alimentación menos nutritiva y la poca actividad física son algunos de los factores que aumentan la probabilidad de la enfermedad de Alzheimer y que puedes abordar. Cambiar alguno de estos factores podría ayudar a retrasar el inicio de la demencia.

Algunas investigaciones sugieren que ciertas vitaminas, como las B12, D y E, y los omega-3 podrían favorecer una buena función cerebral, especialmente en personas con demencia.

Sin embargo, la investigación todavía no ha concluido de forma definitiva que estos u otros suplementos puedan mejorar tu memoria de manera directa.

Aun así, muchas vitaminas tienen múltiples beneficios para la salud. Por eso, llevar una alimentación equilibrada y tomar suplementos, si los necesitas, puede ayudarte a mantenerte saludable en general.

title: Esperanza de vida y pronóstico de la enfermedad de Alzheimer
content:
La esperanza de vida promedio de una persona con la enfermedad de Alzheimer es de 5,8 años tras el diagnóstico. Sin embargo, un diagnóstico temprano puede darte a ti y a tus seres queridos la oportunidad de hacer cambios en el estilo de vida que puedan retrasar o prevenir la aparición de la demencia por Alzheimer.

La enfermedad de Alzheimer (EA) es un trastorno degenerativo del cerebro. Esta enfermedad descompone y destruye las células del cerebro y las neuronas que conectan entre sí las células. Ese daño provoca una disminución de la memoria, del comportamiento y de las capacidades mentales.

El proceso de cada persona con EA es diferente. En algunas personas, la enfermedad avanza lentamente y la función mental permanece en gran medida intacta durante varios años. En otras personas, sobre todo en etapas más avanzadas, la EA es agresiva y les provoca una pérdida de memoria rápida. Con el tiempo, la EA llega a ser lo bastante severa como para afectar la vida diaria. En las etapas finales, las personas necesitan cuidados casi constantes.

Actualmente, la EA es la causa más común de demencia en EE. UU. Según los Centros para el Control y la Prevención de Enfermedades (CDC, por sus siglas en inglés), 5.8 millones de personas en EE. UU. tienen EA. Investigadores y científicos han estudiado esta enfermedad durante décadas, pero por el momento no existe una cura.

Una vez establecido el diagnóstico, la calidad de vida se vuelve cada vez más importante para las personas con EA y para quienes las cuidan.

La esperanza de vida varía en cada persona con EA. Según una revisión sistemática de estudios de 2021, la esperanza de vida promedio de una persona con EA es de 5,8 años tras el diagnóstico.

Recibir un diagnóstico de EA puede llevar mucho tiempo. Si una persona tiene dificultades para pensar o recordar, es posible que le hagan varios tipos de pruebas, como análisis de sangre y de orina, estudios de imagen cerebral y evaluaciones psiquiátricas. Los Institutos Nacionales de la Salud (NIH, por sus siglas en inglés) recomiendan que una persona con problemas de memoria consulte a un médico cada 6 a 12 meses.

En los últimos años, los diagnósticos de EA se han dado más temprano por varias razones. Entre ellas, hay una mayor conciencia sobre esta enfermedad, lo que hace que las personas busquen atención médica antes. También existen pruebas diagnósticas más avanzadas que pueden permitir un diagnóstico más temprano.

Un diagnóstico temprano ofrece la oportunidad de reducir los factores de riesgo y, así, retrasar el inicio de las etapas posteriores de la EA.

Con nuevas pruebas diagnósticas, algunas personas pueden desarrollar signos tempranos de EA y recibir un diagnóstico de enfermedad de Alzheimer antes de presentar síntomas que se clasificarían como demencia.

Un estudio de 2022 con 2.449 personas mayores de 65 años encontró que un estilo de vida saludable —incluido una alimentación nutritiva, el ejercicio y poco o nada de alcohol— puede retrasar la aparición de la demencia en la enfermedad de Alzheimer.

En el estudio, las mujeres con 4 o 5 factores de estilo de vida saludable pasaron, en promedio, 2,6 años con demencia. Las mujeres con 0 o 1 factor saludable pasaron, en promedio, 4,1 años con demencia.

En los hombres, el promedio fue de 1,4 años con demencia cuando tenían 4 o 5 factores de estilo de vida saludable, y de 2,1 años con demencia cuando tenían 0 o 1 factor de estilo de vida saludable.

Al final, la EA avanzará y afectará al cerebro y al cuerpo. A medida que avance, los síntomas empeorarán.

Sin embargo, algunos medicamentos podrían desacelerar la progresión de la EA, al menos durante un breve periodo. El tratamiento también puede mejorar tu calidad de vida y ayudar a tratar los síntomas. Habla con tu médico sobre tus opciones de tratamiento.

Según un estudio de 2020 de la Lancet Commission sobre la prevención de la demencia, modificar 12 factores de riesgo podría retrasar o prevenir hasta el 40% de los diagnósticos de demencia en todo el mundo. Esos cambios en el estilo de vida son:

“El Alzheimer no es una afección que ocurra rápido. Avanza lentamente con el paso del tiempo, por lo general a lo largo de varios años”.

— Kristina D. Carter, PharmD, MBA, MHA

Las citas representan las opiniones de nuestras personas expertas médicas. Todo el contenido es estrictamente informativo y no debe considerarse consejo médico.

Aunque la edad es el principal factor de riesgo conocido para la enfermedad de Alzheimer, esta enfermedad no es una parte necesaria ni inevitable del envejecimiento. La mayoría de las personas diagnosticadas con Alzheimer tienen 65 años o más. La probabilidad de EA casi se duplica cada 5 años a partir de los 65 años. Después de los 85 años, la probabilidad aumenta hasta casi un tercio.

Un diagnóstico temprano brinda la oportunidad de realizar cambios en el estilo de vida que pueden ayudar a reducir los factores de riesgo de demencia. Esos cambios pueden retrasar o prevenir la aparición de síntomas de demencia.

Hay tres etapas de la EA. La mayoría de las personas pasan más tiempo en la etapa intermedia, con síntomas moderados. Los síntomas pueden superponerse entre etapas, así que no hay un punto de corte claro entre una etapa y otra.

La mayoría de las personas se diagnostican en la etapa temprana. Después del diagnóstico, la esperanza de vida promedio es de 5,8 años, pero una persona puede vivir 20 años o más.

En la etapa temprana, las personas presentan:

En esta etapa, se dañan las áreas del cerebro que controlan el lenguaje, el procesamiento sensorial, el razonamiento y el pensamiento consciente. Por lo general, las personas pasan la mayoría de los años después del diagnóstico de EA en esta etapa intermedia. Las personas pueden presentar:

En la etapa tardía de la EA, los cambios en el cerebro provocan que se encoge en varias áreas del cerebro que controlan la memoria, la toma de decisiones, las habilidades de pensamiento y la regulación emocional. Las placas y los ovillos siguen formándose en todo el cerebro, por lo que el funcionamiento disminuye considerablemente. Las personas pueden presentar:

Cada persona tiene un historial de salud único. Ese historial de salud se relaciona directamente con la forma en que la EA le afectará. Puede ser útil conocer las estadísticas sobre la esperanza de vida promedio, así como entender cómo el estilo de vida y la edad pueden modificarla.

Las personas con antecedentes de accidentes cerebrovasculares, traumatismos en la cabeza o enfermedades crónicas severas (como enfermedad cardíaca, enfermedad pulmonar en etapa terminal o cáncer avanzado) pueden presentar un impacto de la EA más grave que las personas que, por lo demás, están saludables.

Si cuidas a alguien o si hace poco te diagnosticaron EA, puedes sentir más fortaleza y tranquilidad al saber cómo suele avanzar esta afección. Esta información puede servirte mientras tú, tu familia y las personas que te cuidan planifican el apoyo a tu salud.

Aquí hay algunas preguntas que muchas personas hacen sobre el Alzheimer y la esperanza de vida:

En las etapas tardías del Alzheimer, las personas experimentan dificultades de movimiento y pueden perder la motivación para moverse. También pueden tener dificultades para tragar y otros desafíos al comer. La pérdida de la comunicación puede dificultarles expresar sus deseos. Estos y otros factores pueden hacer que una persona sea más vulnerable a enfermedades, lo que puede acarrear la muerte.

La gente puede vivir muchos años con un diagnóstico de EA. La demencia es un síntoma de las etapas tardías de la enfermedad de Alzheimer. En un estudio de 2022, las personas vivieron un promedio de 1,4 a 4,1 años con demencia, según el género y los factores de estilo de vida saludable.

Las etapas del Alzheimer varían de una persona a otra. Por lo general, la etapa intermedia, o moderada, de la EA es la que dura más tiempo.

En la etapa tardía, o severa, del Alzheimer, una persona puede depender de otras personas para el cuidado de todos los días. Es posible que ya no pueda comunicarse en absoluto y que pase la mayor parte del tiempo en la cama. En esta etapa, la desnutrición y la falta de movimiento pueden llevar a infecciones y enfermedades que afectan otras partes del cuerpo además del cerebro.

Quizás quieras hablar con tu médico sobre cómo tus factores de riesgo y tu estilo de vida pueden afectar tu esperanza de vida. Trabaja con tu médico para encontrar los mejores tratamientos y cambios en el estilo de vida para tu situación.

Si cuidas a una persona con EA, trabaja con su médico para conocer los tratamientos y los cambios en el estilo de vida que puedan ayudar a desacelerar la progresión de la enfermedad.

La enfermedad de Alzheimer no tiene cura, pero hay medidas que puedes tomar para ayudar a reducir su impacto en la calidad de vida y en las actividades del día a día.

title: Can GLP-1 Medications Slow Down Aging?
content:
Currently, doctors prescribe GLP-1 medications, such as semaglutide and tirzepatide, to help people lose weight and manage type 2 diabetes. However, there’s evidence that these drugs might also help slow biological aging and protect our organs from wear and tear, helping us enjoy more years of good health as we age.

This article will explore how GLP-1 medications might reduce the inflammation that drives aging and how we could use them in the future to improve many aspects of our health.

Biological aging differs from chronological aging, which relates to the passage of time. Biological aging refers to the health of your cells and organs and how well they function. Two 60-year-olds may be the same chronological age, but depending on their lifestyle choices, genetics, and health factors, their biological aging can be very different.

A major driver of aging is chronic (long term) low-grade inflammation. This happens when the immune system is constantly activated, but not enough to cause symptoms, such as pain or fever. 

This type of continuous inflammation can be caused by dietary and lifestyle factors, such as poor sleep, lack of movement, or stress. Over time, it can damage our cells and organs, leading to various chronic diseases.

Evidence is emerging that GLP-1 medications have anti-inflammatory actions that are not simply due to their weight loss effects.

In two large clinical trials, called Sustain and Pioneer, researchers found that semaglutide (the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy) significantly lowered C-reactive protein (CRP) levels. CRP is a marker of inflammation that spikes when your body is under stress or fighting a disease.

The researchers found that weight loss and better blood sugar levels accounted for only about 20% to 60% of the drop in CRP. This suggests that the drug is doing something else to fight inflammation, unrelated to a person’s weight.

To investigate this effect further, researchers conducted the Pioneer 2 trial. They compared people taking semaglutide to people taking a different diabetes drug called empagliflozin (Jardiance). Both groups lost about the same amount of weight (about 4%), but only those on the GLP-1 medication saw their inflammation levels drop by 30%. The other drug had no effect on inflammation, suggesting that GLP-1s are unique in the way they protect the body.

GLP-1 medications might slow down biological aging in the following ways:

GLP-1 medications may reduce inflammation by activating parts of the brain that signal the immune system to stop overreacting to perceived threats. 

This reduces inflammation in organs such as the heart, liver, and kidneys, preventing fibrosis, which is internal scarring that impairs their function as we get older. 

GLP-1 medications may also act on immune cells in the gut and directly on joint tissues, like cartilage, to help keep the body’s structures strong and healthy.

GLP-1 medications may slow the deterioration of cell and tissue health throughout the body. 

In a 2026 study, researchers gave semaglutide (Ozempic, Wegovy) to some participants and a placebo to others for 32 weeks. They found that semaglutide slowed the pace of cellular aging by 9% and lowered biological age scores on special tests. The participants’ cells were behaving like those of a person several years younger.

These effects appear to work independently of weight loss, meaning the drug itself is likely providing these longevity benefits.

Currently, doctors only prescribe GLP-1 medications to help with type 2 diabetes and obesity. The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has not approved them for aging support or longevity, and this would be considered an off-label use.

Although recent research is promising, large, long-term studies are necessary to determine whether prescribing GLP-1 medications is safe and effective for slowing biological aging. 

Most current research is on people who already have obesity or diabetes. To determine whether GLP-1 medications truly promote longevity, researchers need to examine how they affect biological aging in people with a healthy weight.

As experts discover more about the effects of GLP-1 medications, it’s likely that they will be approved for more uses in the future. 

Making healthy lifestyle choices can help reduce inflammation and protect the health of our cells and tissues. While many behaviors can bring benefits, some of the most important steps are: 

Read more: Habits to Form Now for a Longer Life

“Inflammation can show up in a few different ways. It can cause redness, swelling, warmth, or pain, or it can be harder to notice with symptoms like fatigue, achy joints, or getting sick more often than usual. 

Your doctor can run a simple blood test to look for markers that suggest inflammation may be happening in your body. If you’re curious, it’s worth asking about it at your next checkup.”

— Olivia Taylor, PharmD, BCPS

Learn more: CRP Home Tests to Help Gauge Your Health

Quotes represent the opinions of our medical experts. All content is strictly informational and should not be considered medical advice.

GLP-1 medications are showing promising effects for helping slow the rate of biological aging. Research suggests that they reduce the inflammation that can damage our cells and organs over time.

As scientists discover more about these drugs, they may become a useful tool for extending the number of healthy years we have as we age.

title: Foundayo vs. Wegovy: How Do They Compare?
content:
Foundayo (orforglipron) and Wegovy (semaglutide) belong to a class of drugs known as glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonists. GLP-1 medications work by mimicking a natural hormone that regulates appetite in the brain. They tell your body you are full, which helps you eat less and lose weight. 

The drugs do similar things and have similar side effects, but differ in how you take them. The table below compares the main features of Foundayo and Wegovy.

Both drugs are approved for adults with obesity or those who are overweight with at least one weight-related health problem, like high blood pressure. Wegovy also has an extra approval to help reduce the risk of major heart problems, like heart attacks or strokes, in adults with heart disease.

You take Foundayo and Wegovy in different ways. However, for both drugs, you start at a low dose and increase it gradually until you reach a maintenance dose. However, it’s not necessary to increase the dose each month if the current dose is working well for you.

Foundayo is a tablet you swallow once a day, with or without food. The dosage starts at 0.8 milligrams (mg) daily and can go up to 17.2 mg. 

Wegovy was first approved as a once-weekly injection that you give yourself in the stomach, thigh, or arm. However, it’s now also available as a once-daily tablet that you take on an empty stomach with a small amount of water.

Wegovy injections usually start at 0.25 mg once weekly and can be increased to 2.4 mg or 7.2 mg. The tablet version goes from 1.5 mg up to 25 mg daily.

The side effects of Foundayo and Wegovy are similar because they work in the same way. Common side effects are digestion-related, such as:

It’s also possible you’ll experience headaches, fatigue, or hair loss.

The price you’ll pay for Foundayo and Wegovy varies depending on your dosage, how long you take it, the form you use, and the pharmacy that supplies it. 

If you’re considering trying a GLP-1 medication, talk with your doctor about the best option for you.

title: The Most Efficient Momcozy Products for On-The-Go Parents in 2026
content:
Managing the 24/7 demands of parenthood requires gear that works as hard as you do, without sacrificing quality. Momcozy steps in here, as it prioritizes day-to-day efficiency and medical-grade safety and materials. 

While some of these premium essentials can be an investment, events like Amazon Prime Day, which runs from June 23 to June 26 this year, offer discounts that make upgrading your parenting routine much more accessible.

The M5 has been updated: Same breast pump, same quality, but longer battery life and now compatible with an app. You can adjust suction and mode settings through it, as well as track your milk output and visualize your pumping statistics.

This breast pump is available as a single pump, a double pump, and a double breast pump with 120 bags for milk. All options are hands-free and wearable. This pump is BPA-free, made with food-grade silicone, and is heat-safe up to 212°F.

The pump comes with 4 parts and includes a carry case, four different-sized flanges, and a flange ruler.

*Price may vary.

The Air 1 breast pump was made to be ultra-slim for those parents who are truly on the go and need milk to be ready during a commute, on a plane ride, or any other social setting. They're designed to be discreet and wearable.

Its transparent top helps you align faster, so there's no adjusting in between, and you can see the milk output with the clear markings. And if you want to change the setting, you can use the app to adjust the mode and suction seamlessly.

The app also helps you track your milk output and offers auto-mode switching, visual lactation data, and more.

The double breast pump comes with a wireless charging case that can support up to 15 pumping sessions, and each pump collects up to 6 ounces of milk.

This pump comes with 5 parts that are hand-wash only. It includes the wireless charging case, 4 flange inserts, a spare valve, and a flange cover.

What is something parents should keep in mind about breast pumps?

I wish I had learned earlier in my pumping journey how important it is to have the right flange size and that each breast may need a different size.

— Carissa Stephens, R.N., CCRN, CPN

*Price may vary.

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recommends sanitizing and washing your baby's bottle and other items that go into their mouth daily, or more often, to prevent contamination with germs.

If you don't have a dishwasher, sanitizing and washing the bottles can become time consuming as you consider all the steps you have to take to ensure you're being as careful as possible. That's where this cleaning machine comes in.

This machine is specifically designed for baby items and can even be used for wearable breast pump parts. The KleanPal Pro has four main cycles, but you can adjust and combine them to suit your needs.

There are two wash cycles: a rapid wash that lasts 19 minutes and a 'normal' wash cycle that lasts 29 minutes. The steam cycle goes up to 212°F to dry and sterilize the items in the washer, and the storage keeps everything sterile and fresh for up to 72 hours.

The machine itself has 12 jets that are directional, which means they focus on deep-cleaning the inside of the bottles, and 14 other jets swirl around at a high pressure to clean the outside thoroughly as well.

*Price may vary.

Imagine a scenario where you have your baby's milk ready to go in under 10 minutes, no matter where you are. With Momcozy's portable bottle, this becomes a reality.

You can heat up the milk and the water separately, and the large 17-ounce storage can help you make more than one bottle when necessary. The bottle is leak-proof, rechargeable, portable, and can be a lifesaver in a pinch.

However, it is not dishwasher safe, so it needs to be hand-washed carefully, then air-dried.

*Price may vary.

Nursing on the go can be difficult, and with some bras, using wearable pumps can be too noticeable. This nursing bra from Momcozy is made of a flexible material that accommodates hands-free, wearable breast pumps, so you can pump while running errands, traveling, working, and more.

In addition to its versatility and one-clasp wonder, the padding of this bra is sewn into the cups, so you never have to worry about them being misaligned or misplaced after washing.

This bra is also engineered to flex with your changing cup size, giving you wire-free support that stays invisible under clothing.

*Price may vary.

The PureHug Baby Carrier is built to grow with you and your baby through every stage. Accommodating little ones from 7 to 45 lbs, it seamlessly transitions from the newborn days all the way to toddlerhood.

It is made from 100% certified OEKO-TEX cotton, meaning it is incredibly gentle on sensitive skin and actually gets softer with every wash. 

Its adjustable seat, padded leg openings, and ergonomic X-shaped straps safely protect your posture by lifting pressure off your spine and redistributing it evenly across your back.

No matter the job or the routine, parenting is a full-time and never-ending job. We chose these Momcozy products because they try to help make life a little easier for those ‘hurry-up and go’ moments.

title: Your FAQs Answered About When C. diff is an Emergency
content:
Clostridioides difficile (C. diff) is a type of bacteria found in the environment and in the intestines of some healthy people. It can be a part of the gut microbiome, a community of microorganisms that support digestion, immunity, and other important bodily functions.

In a healthy gut, beneficial bacteria help keep potentially harmful bacteria, including C. diff, under control. But when this balance is disrupted, often by antibiotic use, C. diff can multiply rapidly and cause a C. diff infection (CDI).

During a CDI, C. diff releases toxins that damage the lining of the colon, leading to inflammation, ulcers, and fluid loss. As the immune system responds to fight the infection, it releases inflammatory molecules called cytokines. While cytokines help fight the infection, they can also contribute to tissue damage when levels become to high.

Common symptoms of CDI include diarrhea, abdominal pain, cramping, fever, and nausea.

CDI can often be teated at home with guidance from your doctor. However, in some cases, the infection can become severe and lead to serious, potentially life-threatening complications. Knowing when to seek emergency help can get you the care you need as quickly as possible.

If you develop any symptoms of C. diff, it’s important to contact your doctor as soon as possible. Early diagnosis and treatment can help prevent the infection from getting worse or spreading to other people.

Symptoms of C. diff to watch for include:

While these symptoms may not always be an emergency, they should be evaluated by a doctor.

While any suspected C. diff should be evaluated by a doctor, certain symptomsm require immediate medical attention. Seek emergency care if you experience severe symptoms, such as: 

In the emergency room, doctors can evaluate the severity of your infection, check for dehydration, and begin treatment for C. diff.

Prompt treatment is key to reducing the chance of serious complications. CDI can worsen quickly and lead to serious complications. Any new or worsening symptoms are always a reason to seek immediate care.

In some cases, a CDI can lead to serious complications that require emergency medical care, such as:

Seek emergency care right away if you experience any of the following symptoms:

These symptoms may indicate that the infection is progressing or that a serious complication has developed. Prompt treatment can be critical to protecting your health.

If you’re experiencing symptoms of a severe CDI, getting medical care quickly is the top priority. However, bringing a few key pieces of information with you can help your care team evaluate and treat you more efficiently. 

If possible, bring:

Once you’re in the emergency room, your care team may assess your symptoms and look for signs of complications. To confirm or rule out C. diff and determine how severe the infection is, you may need:

Treatment will depend on the severity of your condition. In more serious cases, you may be given intravenous (IV) fluids to treat dehydration and antibiotics that specifically target C. diff. 

You may be admitted to the hospital for close monitoring and ongoing care.

Dehydration is one of the most serious complications of a CDI, especially when severe diarrhea causes the body to lose large amounts of fluids and electrolytes.

As fluid loss increases, your blood volume decreases. This can cause your blood pressure to drop, forcing your heart work harder to circulate blood throughout your body. At the same time, vital organs may receive less oxygen and nutrients than they need to function properly.

Without treatment, severe dehydration can lead to serious complications, including kidney damage, organ dysfunction, and in extreme cases, organ failure.

Dehydration can worsen quickly with CDI. Contact your doctor if diarrhea is severe or persistent, or you’re experiencing signs of dehydration such as dizziness, confusion, or little to no urine output.

If you’ve been diagnosed with CDI and your symptoms get worse or you develop new symptoms, seek medical care right away. In some cases, the infection can progress quickly and lead to serious complications. 

Prompt treatment and careful monitoring can help prevent complications and ensure you receive the care and monitoring needed as your condition changes.

C. diff is a type of bacteria that can exist harmlessly in the gut microbiome of some healthy people. However, when it grows out of control, it can cause a C. diff infection (CDI), leading to symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fever.

While most cases are treatable, C. diff can sometimes become severe. Seek emergency care for symptoms like high fever, severe abdominal pain or swelling, dehydration, or blood in the stool. These could be signs of serious, potentially life-threatening complications. 

Recognizing warning signs early and getting prompt medical attention can help prevent complications and support safe recovery.
